title: A local shop I only found after months
summary: Reflect on a shop or place in your neighbourhood that you discovered late, and what that discovery changed.
topic: A local shop I discovered late
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a shop, café, or place in your neighbourhood that you only discovered after living there for a long time. Describe the place and explain why you had not found it sooner. Use past simple and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: I had lived in the neighbourhood for almost a year before I noticed…
The shop was on a street I never usually walked down because…
When I first went inside, I was surprised to find…
After that, I went there almost every week and…

hintVocab.meaningVi: đồ cũ, đã qua sử dụng

sampleText: I lived in my neighbourhood in Bogotá for almost eight months before I found the bookshop on Calle 23. It was on a narrow side street that I never walked down because my daily route went in a different direction. I only found it because one afternoon it rained suddenly and I ran under the first doorway I saw. The doorway belonged to a small shop full of secondhand Spanish books. The shelves went from the floor to the ceiling, and there was a cat sleeping on a chair near the window. The owner was a short man with glasses who did not look up when I came in. He was reading. I dried off and looked at the books for maybe thirty minutes. I bought three, including one whose cover I liked without reading the description. After that, I went back almost every other week. I liked that it was hard to find. It made me feel like I was the only person who knew about it, even though I was probably the last. Once, I asked the owner how long the shop had been there. He said, 'Forty years.' That was longer than the street had been paved. I thought about all the months I had walked around it without knowing.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: my daily route went in a different direction
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lộ trình hàng ngày của tôi đi theo hướng khác

sampleAnnotations.phrase: ran under the first doorway I saw
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chạy vào mái hiên đầu tiên tôi thấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: shelves went from the floor to the ceiling
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: các kệ chạy từ sàn đến trần nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: did not look up when I came in
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không ngước nhìn khi tôi bước vào

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one whose cover I liked without reading the description
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một cuốn mà tôi thích bìa mà không đọc phần mô tả
gần như mỗi tuần một lần cách tuần
tôi có lẽ là người cuối cùng (biết đến nơi đó)

sampleAnnotations.phrase: how long the shop had been there
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cửa hàng đã ở đó bao lâu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: longer than the street had been paved
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lâu hơn cả khi con đường được trải nhựa
đi quanh nó mà không hay biết

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for a state before the story moment
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'I lived in my neighbourhood for almost eight months before I found the bookshop' dùng past simple cho cả hai vế. Nhưng cuối bài, 'how long the shop had been there' và 'the street had been paved' dùng past perfect vì những sự kiện đó bắt đầu trước khoảnh khắc của câu chuyện và kéo dài đến đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'I lived… for eight months before I found the bookshop' uses past simple for both. But at the end, 'how long the shop had been there' and 'the street had been paved' use past perfect because these states began before the story moment and lasted until it.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi sống trong khu phố ở Bogotá gần tám tháng trước khi tìm thấy hiệu sách trên Calle 23. Nó nằm trên một con đường phụ hẹp mà tôi không bao giờ đi vì lộ trình hàng ngày của tôi đi theo hướng khác. Tôi chỉ tìm thấy nó vì một chiều trời bỗng mưa và tôi chạy vào mái hiên đầu tiên tôi thấy. Mái hiên đó thuộc về một cửa hàng nhỏ đầy sách tiếng Tây Ban Nha đã qua sử dụng. Các kệ chạy từ sàn đến trần nhà, và có một con mèo đang ngủ trên ghế gần cửa sổ. Chủ nhà là một người đàn ông thấp đeo kính, không ngước nhìn khi tôi bước vào. Ông đang đọc sách. Tôi lau khô người và nhìn sách khoảng ba mươi phút. Tôi mua ba cuốn, trong đó có một cuốn mà tôi thích bìa mà không đọc phần mô tả. Sau đó, tôi quay lại gần như mỗi tuần một lần cách tuần. Tôi thích nơi đó vì khó tìm. Nó khiến tôi cảm thấy như mình là người duy nhất biết về nó, dù có lẽ tôi là người biết sau cùng. Một lần, tôi hỏi chủ nhà cửa hàng đã ở đó bao lâu. Ông nói: 'Bốn mươi năm.' Đó là lâu hơn cả khi con đường được trải nhựa. Tôi nghĩ về suốt bao nhiêu tháng tôi đã đi quanh nó mà không hay biết.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about living somewhere for a period before discovering something.
options: I lived there for eight months before I found the bookshop.
I lived there since eight months before I found the bookshop.
I had live there for eight months before I found the bookshop.
I lived there during eight months before I found the bookshop.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For + period' (eight months) is the correct way to express duration. 'Since' marks a start point; 'during' needs a noun event; 'had live' is incorrect past perfect form.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition expresses a period of time (duration)?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct way to say something belonged to a shop.
options: The doorway belonged to a small shop.
The doorway belonged of a small shop.
The doorway has belonged to a small shop.
The doorway was belonging to a small shop.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Belong to' is the fixed prepositional phrase. 'Belong' is a state verb and does not use continuous forms.
mcQuestions.hint: What preposition follows 'belong'? Can 'belong' use the continuous form?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says that shelves reach from the floor to the top?
options: The shelves went from the floor to the ceiling.
The shelves went from floor until ceiling.
The shelves went from the floor until the ceiling.
The shelves went between the floor and ceiling.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'From … to …' is the correct paired preposition for extent. 'Until' implies time, not physical space.
mcQuestions.hint: Which pair of prepositions shows extent from one point to another?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly describes drying off after getting wet.
options: I dried off and looked at the books.
I dryed off and looked at the books.
I dried and looked at the books.
I dried me off and looked at the books.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Dried off' (past of 'dry off') is the natural phrasal verb for removing surface moisture. 'Dryed' is a misspelling; 'dried' alone is correct but less idiomatic here.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the correct past tense spelling of 'dry'? Is there a reflexive pronoun needed?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct indirect question form about duration.
options: I asked how long had the shop been there.
I asked how long the shop has been there.
I asked how long the shop had been there.
I asked how long was the shop there.
mcQuestions.explanation: In an indirect question, use statement word order: 'how long the shop had been there'. Direct questions invert; indirect questions do not.
mcQuestions.hint: In a reported question, does the word order stay like a direct question?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'probably' to express a guess?
options: I was probably the last person to find it.
I was probably the most last person to find it.
I was probably last person to find it.
I was probably the later person to find it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The last person' needs the definite article. 'Most last' is redundant; 'later' is a comparative, not a superlative.
mcQuestions.hint: Does a superlative like 'last' need the article 'the'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly describes going somewhere on an alternating schedule.
options: I went back almost every other week.
I went back almost every others week.
I went back almost every two weeks.
I went back almost each other week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every other week' means once every two weeks (alternating). 'Every two weeks' is also correct but not as natural here. 'Each other week' is non-standard.
mcQuestions.hint: What phrase means 'once in every two weeks'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'stumble across' correctly to mean finding something by chance.
options: I stumbled across the shop by accident.
I stumbled the shop across by accident.
I stumbled across in the shop.
I stumbled to across the shop.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stumble across something' (or 'stumble upon') means to find unexpectedly. The preposition 'across' follows the verb; the object follows 'across'.
mcQuestions.hint: In 'stumble across', does the object come before or after 'across'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'linger' to mean staying in a place for a long time?
options: I liked to linger in the shop for an hour.
I liked to linger at the shop for an hour.
I liked lingering the shop for an hour.
I liked to linger the shop for an hour.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Linger in a place' is the natural collocation. 'Linger the shop' (without a preposition) is incorrect.
mcQuestions.hint: What preposition naturally follows 'linger' when describing a place?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the word that best describes a place that is difficult to reach or find.
options: dusty
secondhand
hidden
regular
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Hidden' means not easily seen or found. 'Dusty' means covered in dust; 'secondhand' means used before; 'regular' means normal or frequent.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means 'not visible or easy to find'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct sentence about thinking about something you missed earlier.
options: I thought about all the months I had walked around it without knowing.
I thought about all the months I walked around it without knowing.
I thought about all the months I have walked around it without knowing.
I thought for all the months I had walked around it without knowing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had walked' (past perfect) correctly shows that the months of walking happened before the moment of thinking about them. 'About' is the correct preposition with 'think' when expressing reflection.
mcQuestions.hint: Did the walking happen before or after the moment of thinking?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever lived somewhere for a long time and then suddenly noticed something that had always been there? What does it feel like to discover you missed something obvious?
